title: [lora_l2.c:1010-1015] lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback silently fails on UNINIT state
description: Critic: Silent failure pattern. What: lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback() returns void but silently fails (logs warning only) when called in UNINIT state. Callers have no way to know the callback was not registered. Why it matters: Caller may proceed assuming callback is active, leading to lost packets. Code at lichen_l2.c:1058 calls this before start() but after init(), so UNINIT case is unlikely but not impossible in edge cases. Fix: Either return int error code, or assert/LOG_ERR on UNINIT since it indicates caller bug. The warn-and-continue pattern hides programming errors.
close_reason: Invalid - issue description is factually incorrect. The function lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback() returns int (not void) and returns -ENODEV when called in UNINIT state. Callers can and should check the return value. See lora_l2.h declaration and lora_l2.c:1103-1107 implementation.
